commands.review_summary:
The PR adds WhatsApp support for shared `message(action="upload-file")` media sends by advertising the action, lowering path/URL uploads through `sendMessageWhatsApp`, preserving forced-document bytes, and adding regression coverage plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main is enough: WhatsApp does not currently support `upload-file`, so a shared message action resolved to WhatsApp reaches plugin dispatch and returns the runner's unsupported-action error.
commands.review_followup: This is a maintainer-labeled MEMBER PR with no narrow ClawSweeper repair needed; the next action is explicit maintainer review, body cleanup, and normal merge gating.

target.title: fix(whatsapp): lower upload-file media sends
target.body:
## Summary

- Lower WhatsApp-targeted `message(action="upload-file")` calls with path/URL media into the existing `send` + `media` path.
- Keep WhatsApp's native action discovery unchanged; this only handles shared-message-tool calls that resolve to WhatsApp at execution time.
- Reject buffer-only WhatsApp upload-file payloads loudly because they cannot be safely represented by the send-media transport.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- Live source-install WhatsApp check with a configured WhatsApp channel, using `message(action="upload-file", channel="whatsapp", filePath="/Users/guti/.openclaw/media/outbound/openclaw-wa-upload-test.md")`

Behavior addressed: A shared `message` tool call using `action=upload-file` with a media path/URL no longer fails after resolving to WhatsApp; it is delivered through WhatsApp's supported send-media path.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout with targeted Vitest coverage and changed-file validation; remote source install running this branch with a configured WhatsApp channel.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts`; `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`; `pnpm check:changed`; install branch into a source checkout, restart the gateway, then send a WhatsApp prompt that calls the shared `message` tool once with `action=upload-file`, `channel=whatsapp`, and a Markdown file under OpenClaw's managed media directory.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests pass for WhatsApp upload-file path lowering and buffer rejection, existing WhatsApp action discovery tests remain unchanged, and the live WhatsApp prompt delivered the Markdown attachment from the managed media directory.

Observed result after fix: Targeted tests report 2 passed files / 35 passed tests, Plugin SDK API baseline check reports OK, `pnpm check:changed` completes successfully, and the configured WhatsApp chat receives the uploaded Markdown file.

What was not tested: Buffer-only upload-file payloads are intentionally rejected and covered by tests; live WhatsApp proof used a managed local Markdown path rather than a buffer payload.
target.author: ngutman
